headline: Design Systems Meet Autonomous AI
subheadline: Today's picks connect the dots between your MCP work, the evolving role of design leaders, and where vibe coding tools actually stand in 2026.

sections.label: Watch
sections.title: Design with Claude Code: The Designer's Guide
sections.description: This is specifically made for designers (not devs) using Claude Code with Figma MCP in real product workflows. You're already deep in this world, but it's worth scanning for any workflow patterns or MCP tricks you haven't tried. 8.8K views and growing — it's clearly resonating with the design community.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: Automating Design Systems with AI: 2026 Workflow Guide
sections.description: This one's directly in your lane — it covers how MCP lets Figma send structured data to AI tools, specifically for automating design system tasks. Given you're building exactly this for your company's white-label sport apps, there might be architectural ideas or patterns worth stealing.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: Product Design in the Age of AI: What Needs to Change in 2026
sections.description: Speaks to the shift you're living — from siloed design/dev/PM roles toward cross-functional product teams where design leaders need to think in systems AND AI capabilities. Relevant as you think about what your next role looks like: product builder, design leader, or something entirely new.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: 17 AI Tools Every Product Manager Needs in 2026
sections.description: Not a PM listicle — it specifically calls out Figma's MCP integrations and Figma Make as tools that matter for PMs. Useful lens for you: understanding how product managers are starting to interact with the design system layer you're building. Great for thinking about your work's cross-functional impact.

sections.label: Trending
sections.title: Vibe Coding Tool Landscape Has Officially Exploded
sections.description: The consensus solidifying across multiple comparisons: Lovable for rapid validation, Cursor for production code, Claude Code for complex multi-file work. You're already using all three — which puts you ahead of most designers. The interesting signal: Google Antigravity is now in these conversations too.

sections.label: Think about this
sections.title: Designers will move from creating products to designing systems
sections.description: One of five predictions for design in 2026: the highest-value design work shifts from crafting individual screens to architecting the systems that AI agents use to generate them. You're literally doing this with your MCP + design system project. Worth sitting with: is the system you're building designed to be consumed by AI, not just humans?

sections.label: Concept of the day
sections.title: AI Agents as Digital Teammates
sections.description: Product Hunt's AI agents category is booming — agents for engineering, design, sales, ops. The mental model shift: these aren't tools you use, they're teammates you delegate to. As someone curious about AI agents but early on, browse this page to see what's shipping. The design agent category is especially young and ripe.
